title: Welcome to China
body: Hello and welcome to the Shanghai International Circuit, ahead of the first and only practice session of the weekend...

title: Just one practice session today
body: This is going to be a tough ask of the teams - one hour of practice, before Sprint Qualifying a little later on. It will be a busy session, with the teams needing to learn the track, where to harvest energy, where to brake and how some different Pirelli compounds handle here.
No one can afford any reliability woes in this session, that is for sure.

title: Russell the man to beat
body: George Russell seems to be the man to catch here - based on a race sample of one. Mercedes were fast over one lap and in the race last weekend, with Russell leading home his team mate.
But the race might have been closer, had Ferrari nailed their strategy.

Be that as it may, Russell was eight tenths of a second ahead of his nearest non-Mercedes rival in Qualifying last weekend, so catching up to the Silver Arrows over one lap will be the first challenge for the rest.

title: Antonelli in the mix
body: Kimi Antonelli impressed in Australia - but only after he crashed in FP3. That hampered his Qualifying, and yet he grabbed P2 on the grid and wound up finishing in that slot too.

Considering his car was never set-up properly in Qualifying, it was an impressive effort from the youngster. Ditto in the race, where a bad start dropped him down the order, only for the teenager to climb back through the field.
Can he take the fight to Russell, week in, week out? That is the question, and we should start to see an answer this weekend.

title: Ferrari challenging hard
body: Ferrari seem to be the closest challengers for Mercedes so far, with both Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton looking quick last weekend.

The good news for the Scuderia is that both their drivers are happy and relaxed at the moment. Leclerc is still basking in the afterglow of his wedding, while Hamilton says he is a rejuvenated figure in 2026.
And they say they did not optimise their package fully in Australia, so they should be able to get closer here.

title: Ferrari to run innovative ‘flip-flop’ wing in FP1 at the Chinese GP
That's the green light
body: FP1 is underway here in China, on a bit of a chilly if sunny morning. There is a queue at the end of the pit lane, plenty of drivers very keen to get going out there.

title: A view from the paddock
body: "We've got another bright, clear day at the Shanghai International Circuit, but that doesn't mean it's particularly warm! Early morning and late afternoon temperatures have been rather chilly so far this week, meaning jackets are out in force in the build-up to FP1," says F1.com's Mike Seymour.

"A hugely important hour of practice lies ahead for the teams and drivers as they try to dial in their all-new cars – at a completely different track to Melbourne's Albert Park – before going straight into a competitive session.
"Ferrari also have the added task of running their so-called 'flip-flop' rear wing in FP1. Could it help them close the gap to early pace-setters Mercedes? We'll soon find out..."

title: The tyres in China
body: Pirelli have gone one step harder here than in Melbourne with the C2, C3 and C4 rubber.
The teams get 12 sets in total for Sprint weekends, not the usual 13 so they need to watch those allocations carefully - and the drivers need to avoid costly lock ups today.

title: Colapinto has a spin
body: The cameras pick up an Alpine facing the wrong way, it is Colapinto who appears to have had a spin. He waits for a few drivers to go past, before getting his car back pointing the right way.
Replays show an unusual spin, he wasn't really into the corner yet - the car just let go on him, similar to Verstappen's spin last time out in Australia.

title: Norris and Hamilton have a moment
body: What is going on down there? Norris goes to overtake Hamilton, who at the last moment decides he wants track position and tries to stay ahead. Those two wind up making minor contact.
And now replays show a big spin for Hamilton, after his brakes locked up. His tyres are all sporting flat spots now.

title: Most on the mediums
body: As for the tyres, the whole field are on the mediums bar Aston Martin, who have sent both drivers out on softs.

It remains to be seen what programme Alonso and Stroll will run today, as their team is still short on parts. As such, race distance might be tricky on Sunday, but completing the Sprint will be one aim for the team as that is just 19 laps here.

title: TURN 16 INCIDENT INVOLVING CARS 1 (NOR) AND 44 (HAM) NOTED - CAUSING A COLLISION
Russell leads the way
body: Early days in the session, but Russell leads by just 0.029s from his team mate. So, Mercedes to the fore again then.
Leclerc is just under half a second back in third, ahead of Gasly and Piastri.

title: The VSC comes out
body: There is a brief VSC period for some debris on the straight, which is collected by the marshals. Replays show Bortoleto locked up and ran wide, but the debris does not appear to be from his car.
The incidents are thick and fast - Hadjar is now waving his hands at a Cadillac, that caused him some traffic out there.

time: Former F1 Driver on F1 TV
title: Jolyon Palmer
body: It was always going to be a trade-off with this [Ferrari] ‘flip’ rear wing. The car will have a bigger top speed, but what we’re seeing is the drivers are going to have to manually deactivate it earlier. It’s not going to be the most efficient way under braking.

title: Problems for Lindblad
body: Uh oh - the yellow flags are out, and it is for Lindblad at Turn 9. He appears to have stopped out there... And now the VSC is out.
Lindblad has parked up on the inside of the hairpin by the looks of things so that should be easy to push out of the way.

title: Lindblad on the back foot
body: No one can afford to miss a session here, but certainly not the only rookie in the field. Lindblad does not have much experience of this track bar running in the simulator, as F2 and F3 do not tend to run here in the support series.
He is out of the car, as the low loader reverses and starts to get towards that stricken Racing Bulls.
As such, the VSC is still in place for the time being.

title: The track is clear
body: The VSC has ended now, so the drivers can get back to doing what they do best - driving fast. Russell remains on top of the pile, but now Leclerc is second ahead of Antonelli and Bearman, the Haas man impressing in Melbourne last week.
Sainz is another man missing valuable running here, he is in the pits having completed just the one lap.

title: A reminder of the format for the weekend
body: While the drivers get back up to speed, let's run through how a Sprint weekend unfolds.
Sprint Qualifying later on is a shorter version of Qualifying, the three segments quicker at 12, 10 and eight minutes long.
You also have to use medium tyres in SQ1 and SQ2.
Tomorrow in place of FP3 is the Sprint, a 19 lap dash with no mandatory pit stops.
Then Qualifying is in its usual place in the afternoon, before the race on Sunday.
As such, Sprint Qualifying can inform the teams for Qualifying, and likewise the Sprint for tyre degradation for the race.

title: All eyes on Ferrari's rear wing
body: Ferrari are running that innovative flip-flop wing here in China, and it seemed to cause some of the instability that possibly hampered Hamilton earlier on when he spun.

Or was it a coincidence? Ferrari certainly won't be telling anyone, but it does seem to take a fraction longer to shut that a normal rear wing with Active Aero. But when we say fraction, we are talking thousandths of a second if that.
Either way, it will be fascinating to see their top line speed down the straights compared to some of the other teams here.

title: Hamilton swaps to softs
body: Hamilton has given up on his flat-spotted medium tyres, and bolted on some softs. But his first lap is not stunning, and he slots into fifth place.
He grabbed pole for the Sprint at this venue last season, and won the Sprint to boot - thus far, his only Ferrari victory. Can he repeat that feat this weekend?
His old team Mercedes will certainly have something to say about that.

title: No one else makes the switch yet
body: As Hamilton continues to push on the softs, everyone else remains on their original mediums bar Stroll - Aston Martin running a very different programme to the rest of the field today.
Hamilton improves to third, by he is still four and a half tenths off Russell whose marker lap was set on the mediums.

title: Sainz remains in the pits
body: This is not ideal for Williams and Sainz, the Spaniard getting to worst of the reliability luck in Australia too.
Reports are that it is a data issue that is keeping Sainz in the garage at the moment, and he remains stuck on just the one lap in this session.

title: Lindblad out of the session
body: This one is not surprising, given his car remains behind the barriers at Turn 14, but Racing Bulls have confirmed that Lindblad is out of the session.
He managed six laps before stopping with a mechanical issue - better than nothing, but only just.
He impressed in Australia in making Q3, if he makes SQ3 today it will be arguably an even more impressive performance from the fearless youngster.

title: Hamilton climbs to second
body: Hamilton has improved to second place now, 0.208s back on Russell. But again, the Mercedes man set his time on the mediums, not the softs. We are still awaiting the rest of the field swapping tyres - Hamilton was forced to do so early after spinning and flat-spotting his mediums.
And speaking of waiting, Sainz is waiting no more - he is finally out on track.

title: The track remains busy
body: As predicted, this session has been fairly frantic with the drivers and teams trying to squash as much learning into this hour as possible. Most have completed some slightly higher fuel runs now, with Russell and Leclerc in the pits having taken the best out of their medium tyres.
Piastri is back out on his mediums, and so is Norris who once again seems a fraction of his team mate here so far.

title: Russell on softs as Colapinto stops
body: Just as Russell bolts on the softs for some quick runs, there is an issue in the pit lane. Colapinto has come to a halt in the fast lane in the pits. The marshals are covering that will yellow flags, allowing everyone else to undertake the Argentine.
Now as the Alpine mechanics run down the pit lane, he finally gets going, at least pulling out of the fast lane to park up out of the way.

title: Quick laps incoming
body: Russell has set a 1m 32.807s to remain in the top spot. Antonelli goes second, 0.156s back so that is a tidy effort from the youngster.
Leclerc pops into third ahead of his team mate, but he is nearly eight tenths off Russell's pace.

title: Lewis Hamilton
body: Give me a plan mate, I don't know where I am in the session!

title: All eyes on McLaren
body: Mercedes look good, Ferrari are challenging them - but about last season's champions though?
Both Piastri and Norris are on personal best push laps on their first flying run on the softs.
Norris climbs to fourth, a tenths back on Leclerc - that seems to be an improvement on last week.
Piastri abandoned his lap after a mistake in the middle sector.

title: Bearman beats Verstappen
body: Haas have come to play here, with Bearman - who headed the midfield in Australia - in sixth just behind Hamilton.
He is 0.029s ahead of Verstappen, who slots into seventh ahead of Piastri who is now on a second push lap.
And the Australian jumps up to third, 0.736s back on Russell but a tenth ahead of Leclerc.

title: Hadjar on his first push run
body: The clock is ticking down - what can Hadjar do? He was another youngster to impress last time out, before unluckily retiring from the race with a PU issue.
Not much is the answer, he climbs from 18th to 14th. Time to find for the Frenchman, he is 2.444s back from Russell's leading marker.

title: Out comes the chequered flag
body: FP1 draws to a close, although there are a few drivers still on a lap. Hulkenberg has popped into ninth at a track he does tend to go well at, ahead of Gasly who is just about in the top 10.
But Leclerc runs wide through the final corner and makes a trip through the gravel - the Ferrari man is lucky to make it back to the track in one piece there.

title: Russell tops FP1
body: So, Russell winds up on top of the pile for Mercedes, with a 1m 32.741s. He was 0.120s ahead of his team mate, with Norris improving late on to grab P3 ahead of his team mate.
Ferrari were down in fifth and sixth, Red Bull have Verstappen in eighth and Hadjar in P13.

title: Bearman has a late spin
body: Bearman was fast in FP1, winding up in the top 10. But he did suffer a late spin there, whilst being pursued by an Aston Martin. It looked like the traffic in his mirrors was perhaps a tad distracting and he missed his braking point - but at the end of the session, he does not need those tyres again anyway.

title: Two drivers on the back foot
body: Lindblad managed only six laps today, after he stopped out on track with a cockpit full of smoke. Losing so much running is not good for the rookie, who has next to no experience of this track.
Sainz also lost some time with a data issue on his car, but did get out later on and managed 18 laps in the end.

title: Mercedes the team to beat
body: So, as they were in Melbourne, it looks like Mercedes are the team to beat. Russell wound up 0.555s faster than the nearest non-Mercedes car, so another mighty margin for the rest to overcome.

It might be down to Antonelli to challenge his team mate here then, and he did well - winding up 0.120s back, having set his time on his second push lap on the softs and thus having left some fractions out there.

title: Alan Permane
body: Not sure until we get [Lindblad's car] back, it switched off - telemetry, steering wheel. It could be something as simple as a switch, or a change everything.
Fingers crossed it is something simple.

title: FP1: Russell sets the pace from Antonelli and Norris
'A good chance of being in the top 10'
body: Permane also said his team have a "good chance of being in the top 10," here, which bodes well for fans of Lawson and Lindblad.

As to that midfield pecking order, it seems there is a group of Racing Bulls, Audi, Haas and Alpine who are fighting for those last top 10 slots. As such, that section of the grid is fiercely competitive.
Williams are slightly further back at the moment, ahead of Cadillac and Aston Martin.

title: Sprint Qualifying up next
body: So, the drivers now have a short break before leaving it all on the line in Sprint Qualifying.
Russell and Antonelli are once again the class of the field, but the scene behind is not so clear. McLaren pipped Ferrari, but did set their lap times later when the track was rubbered in.
Bearman was seventh, once again impressing for Haas with Audi also in the mix for the top 10.

But Red Bull are a fraction back - Verstappen was only eighth, a huge 1.8s back from Russell's marker. Hadjar struggled in P13, so he needs to find some time here as he has fallen three tenths behind his team mate.

As for the rest, there were plenty of spins with these cars still tricky to handle with grip at a premium in some corners. Sainz, Colapinto and Lindblad all suffered reliability issues, so there is work to do for Williams, Alpine and Racing Bulls in the break.
All of which leaves us to tell you that Sprint Qualifying is at 15:30 local time, 07:30 UTC and we will be back half an hour before for all the build up to what is sure to be a very busy session!
